title: Covering a fruit cake with marzipan
subtitle: Applying a smooth marzipan layer before royal icing or fondant
excerpt: Marzipan goes on a fruit cake before the outer icing, filling in gaps and giving the fondant or royal icing a level base. The cake needs a thin coat of apricot jam to hold the marzipan, and the marzipan must dry for at least 24 hours before the outer coat goes on.
sourceNotes: Marzipan as a base coat for iced fruit cakes is documented in British baking guides from the nineteenth century. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136, describes the application of almond paste to Christmas and wedding cakes before the application of royal icing. The use of sieved apricot jam as an adhesive between the cake surface and the marzipan is a standard technique documented across all subsequent British confectionery references. Florence White, Good Things in England (1932), records the same method as standard British practice.

content.text: The marzipan layer serves two purposes: it fills any dips in the cake's surface and provides a stable, flat base for the final icing. A lumpy marzipan layer produces a lumpy iced cake. Take the time to get it level.

Place the fruit cake upside-down on a board so the flat base becomes the top. Warm the {{apricot-jam}} in a small pan, sieve out any fruit pieces, and brush the entire surface of the cake. The jam should be hot enough to flow and coat easily. Let it cool and become tacky before applying the marzipan.

Dust the work surface with {{icing-sugar}}. Roll the {{marzipan}} into a circle large enough to cover the top and sides in one piece: diameter of the cake plus twice its height, plus a few centimetres of margin. For a 23 cm cake, 8 cm tall, that is about 42 cm. Roll to 5 mm thickness. Lift the marzipan over the rolling pin, lay it over the cake, and press it down the sides, smoothing with your hands. Trim the excess at the base with a sharp knife.
Smooth and dry, at least 24 hours

Use the cake smoother in firm circular strokes to press out any air bubbles and even the surface. Run the smoother along the sides to create a clean vertical join at the base. Leave the cake uncovered in a cool, dry kitchen. Read the
note: the surface must be completely dry before fondant or royal icing is applied. In a cold kitchen, allow 48 hours.
